title: Always put `co_return` at end of coroutine (#6767)
body:
Fixes #6767.

That specific issue was an `always` marked as a coroutine as a workaround to enable the option to `process::kill` it.
It had no timing controls, so no `co_await`s, so the emitted function ended up not being a coroutine after all.
While I think this was the last hole to plug in terms of this bug, I cannot guarantee it.
Thus I decided to simplify the code overall and just put `co_return;` at the end of all coroutines.
